description:
You are a system administrator who must perform a very quick health-check of an external REST
service that one of your servers depends on.  
Use the public testing API at https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com (no authentication
required).  
Your job is to run three small curl commands and then create a single plain-text log
summarising the key results.

Required steps (exactly three HTTP operations):

1. GET request  
   • URL: https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1  
   • You only need the HTTP status code and the JSON field "title".

2. POST request  
   • URL: https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts  
   • Request body (raw JSON, exactly as shown):  
     {"title":"adminCheck","body":"Server maintenance","userId":42}  
   • Content-Type header must be application/json; charset=utf-8  
   • Capture the HTTP status code and the returned JSON field "id".

3. HEAD request  
   • URL: https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts  
   • Capture the HTTP status code and the value of the Content-Type response header.

When you have run the three commands, create the file  
/home/user/api_test.log  
containing exactly six lines, nothing more, nothing less, in the precise order below:

Line 1  GET_STATUS:<status-code>  
Line 2  GET_TITLE:<title-string>  
Line 3  POST_STATUS:<status-code>  
Line 4  POST_ID:<id-value>  
Line 5  HEAD_STATUS:<status-code>  
Line 6  HEAD_CONTENT_TYPE:<content-type-string>

Formatting rules that will be automatically verified:

• Upper-case keys, exactly as shown.  
• Single colon “:” delimiter, no surrounding spaces.  
• No extra spaces or blank lines anywhere in the file.  
• Values must match exactly what the HTTP responses return.

Example (DO NOT copy; produce real values from the live calls):

GET_STATUS:200  
GET_TITLE:some title here  
POST_STATUS:201  
POST_ID:123  
HEAD_STATUS:200  
HEAD_CONTENT_TYPE:application/json; charset=utf-8

Save the log, confirm its contents with cat, and you are done.
truth:
Expected content of /home/user/api_test.log after correct execution:

GET_STATUS:200
GET_TITLE:sunt aut facere repellat provident occaecati excepturi optio reprehenderit
POST_STATUS:201
POST_ID:101
HEAD_STATUS:200
HEAD_CONTENT_TYPE:application/json; charset=utf-8
name: task_000000_a0490b81
